channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistan launches EO-3 earth observation satellite with advanced imaging and AI capabilities
item.description: Pakistan has successfully launched its indigenous electro-optical satellite, EO-3, from China’s Taiyuan Satellite Launch Centre, marking a key development in the country’s growing space programme. Acc...

item.title: Iran rejects maximalist demands as Pakistan leads peace efforts in the conflict
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi has clearly outlined the country's stance on the ongoing conflict as peace talks take place in Islamabad. The meeting, which included discussions with Pakistan’s ...

item.title: Investigation into the Disappearances of US Scientists and Related Speculations
item.description: Recent reports of the disappearances and deaths of at least 11 US scientists, many with backgrounds in space, defence, and nuclear research, have sparked a wave of conspiracy theories in the United St...

item.title: Social media influencer killed in collision outside Soho nightclub
item.description: Klaudia Zakrzewska, a 32-year-old social media influencer from Essex, has tragically died following a collision with a car outside a nightclub on Argyll Street in Soho, central London, at 04:30 BST on...

item.title: President Samia Takes Steps to Promote Unity and Accountability in Tanzania
item.description: Few days after the Presidential Commission on the October 29, 2025 General Election unrest released its report, Ambassador Lazaro Nyalandu, former Minister for Natural Resources and Tourism, said the ...

item.title: Pick n Pay tests ‘GO’ convenience store format in high-traffic South African locations
item.description: Pick n Pay has started piloting a new convenience store format, called Pick n Pay GO, in South Africa after seeing encouraging results from its earlier rollout in Botswana. The concept is designed to ...

item.title: Mamata Banerjee to take legal action against Amit Shah over controversial remarks
item.description: In the midst of the ongoing West Bengal assembly elections, Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has expressed strong objection to Union Minister Amit Shah's recent statements, announcing plans to file a le...

item.title: Gold Coast penalised 50 metres due to confusion over AFL stand rule
item.description: During Saturday’s AFL clash in Tasmania, Gold Coast Suns coach Damien Hardwick expressed frustration after a controversial 50-metre penalty was awarded against his team, giving Hawthorn a crucial goal...

item.title: Kenyan Lady Becomes US Airman After Hard Work and Faith
item.description: Shiko Hope Wa Njeri, a Kenyan content creator living in Dallas, Texas, has achieved a major milestone by becoming a United States Airman. A winner of the Diversity Visa (DV) lottery, Shiko shared her ...

item.title: Maliwal Accuses Kejriwal of Hypocrisy After AAP Exit
item.description: Former AAP leader Swati Maliwal has launched a scathing attack on Arvind Kejriwal following a recent rebellion within the party and a mass exodus from the Rajya Sabha. Maliwal, now aligned with the BJ...

item.title: Plateau State Tertiary Institutions Announce Indefinite Strike Over Unmet Staff Demands
item.description: The Joint Union of Plateau State-owned Tertiary Institutions (JUPTI), wey be umbrella of staff unions for state-owned higher institutions, don announce say dem go commence indefinite strike if governm...

item.title: Marc Márquez wins Spanish GP sprint despite rain and crash, Brad Binder fourth
item.description: In a thrilling display of skill and resilience, Ducati rider Marc Márquez secured a remarkable victory at the Spanish Grand Prix sprint on Saturday, despite a crash early in the race. The event unfold...

item.title: Police and Comelec strengthen security measures for BARMM election candidacy filing
item.description: Authorities in Maguindanao del Sur, together with the Commission on Elections (Comelec), are stepping up preparations to ensure a peaceful and orderly filing of certificates of candidacy (COCs) for th...

item.title: Global report warns persistent conflict, drought and funding cuts will worsen food insecurity in 2026
item.description: A new Global Report on Food Crises warns that hunger will remain at critical levels in 2026, driven by ongoing conflict, climate-related shocks such as drought, and a sharp decline in humanitarian fun...

item.title: Impact of UK Labour challenges on upcoming Scotland and Wales elections
item.description: As elections approach in Scotland and Wales, Labour faces growing challenges under Sir Keir Starmer's leadership. In Wales, Labour's long dominance appears threatened, with disillusionment among voter...

item.title: INEC Redeploys Onuoha as Akwa Ibom REC Despite Concerns Over Political Ties
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) don redeploy Professor Anugbum Onuoha make he serve as the new Resident Electoral Commissioner (REC) for Akwa Ibom State. This change happen after ...

item.title: Macron Focuses on Reopening Strait of Hormuz Amid Global Energy Concerns
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron has emphasised his commitment to reopening the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial passage for global oil and gas shipments, which has been disrupted due to the ongoing US-Isr...

item.title: Bang Si-Hyuk avoids arrest after prosecutors reject warrant request due to lack of evidence
item.description: Bang Si-Hyuk, the founder of the entertainment company HYBE, narrowly avoided an arrest after prosecutors dismissed the request for a warrant, citing insufficient evidence to proceed with legal action...

item.title: Former USF Student Charged With Killing Roommate and His Girlfriend After Disappearance
item.description: A 26-year-old former University of South Florida student, Hisham Abugharbieh, has been charged with two counts of first-degree premeditated murder in connection with the deaths of his roommate, Zamil ...

item.title: British Columbia suspect faces charges linked to extortion-driven shootings in Peel Region
item.description: A man from British Columbia has been charged in connection with a series of shootings in Ontario’s Peel Region that investigators say are tied to an alleged extortion scheme. Police believe the incide...
